name: HashiCorp Nomad API
what_it_does: HashiCorp Nomad's REST API enables programmatic scheduling, management, and monitoring of containerized and non-containerized workloads across a cluster, including job submission, allocation inspection, node management, and ACL-based access control.
use_cases: Submit a new job spec to the cluster to deploy a containerized service, then poll allocation status until healthy
Inspect running allocations for a job to retrieve their logs, resource utilization, and current task states
Scale a job's task group up or down by updating the count field in response to load metrics detected by an agent
List all nodes in the cluster and their health status to identify degraded nodes that need maintenance
Stop and garbage-collect a batch job after its completion is confirmed, freeing cluster resources
not_for: Kubernetes-native ecosystems — Nomad has a different scheduling model and is not a drop-in Kubernetes replacement
Complex multi-tenant environments requiring Kubernetes RBAC, network policies, and rich ecosystem integrations
Teams that need the broad vendor support and tooling ecosystem that has grown around Kubernetes
best_when: An agent needs to orchestrate diverse workloads (containers, binaries, JVM apps, batch jobs) across a cluster without the operational overhead of Kubernetes.
avoid_when: Your infrastructure is already standardized on Kubernetes and switching orchestrators would create more complexity than benefit.

auth.notes: Nomad uses ACL tokens with associated policies that grant specific capabilities (submit-job, read-logs, etc.) on namespaces. Anonymous access can be enabled for read-only operations in trusted environments. Tokens are passed via X-Nomad-Token header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open-source Nomad is fully free with no cluster size or feature limits on core scheduling; HCP Nomad is available as a managed control plane
pricing.paid_tiers: Nomad Enterprise adds multi-region federation, resource quotas, audit logging, Sentinel policy enforcement, and namespace isolation
pricing.notes: Self-hosted open-source is entirely free. Enterprise features require a HashiCorp license agreement.

performance.rate_limits: No documented hard rate limits for the REST API; throughput is constrained by cluster leader capacity; large job submission batches should be spaced to avoid Raft pressure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns plain text or JSON error messages depending on the endpoint; allocation failure reasons are surfaced in task event logs rather than the top-level response, requiring agents to drill down.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job registration is idempotent when the same JobID is submitted — Nomad will create a new version. Allocation operations are not idempotent. Deregistration is safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Nomad uses a blocking query pattern with ?index= and ?wait= parameters for long-polling — polling without these will cause high CPU usage on both client and server
Job evaluation status 'complete' does not mean allocations are healthy — agents must separately check allocation and task states
Namespace support must be explicitly enabled; forgetting to include ?namespace= in queries returns only the default namespace silently
Log retrieval requires streaming the /v1/client/fs/logs endpoint against the specific client node hosting the allocation, not the server
ACL bootstrap is a one-time operation — losing the bootstrap token requires Vault integration or manual recovery

security_score_components.security_notes: ACL policies support fine-grained capability grants per namespace. Native Vault integration enables secrets injection into task environments. TLS mutual authentication is supported for inter-node communication.
